author: peter.davies
content:
I think this case has been causing a lot of hassle to deal with an edge case that is impossible for all practical purposes. This is definitely better than EIP-7610 (which I don't thing Erigon & Reth can even implement without DB changes), but some people will argue this is taking a sledgehammer to a nut.

IIRC all the accounts involved were created by weird pre-Homestead behaviour around `CREATE` reverts where accounts were partially created (see EIP-2).

Why does the EIP title say "Remove" when the EIP actually just bumps the nonce on these accounts?

author: jochembrouwer
content: The title is a working title and it is confusing, thanks for pointing this out

author: jochembrouwer
content: I see the point but I am not comfortable with these old accounts laying around causing a theoretical issue. I can see why EIP-7610 is not nice and am also getting convinced that this specific check should not be part of protocol. But I am also not comfortable with accounts laying around where we have a situation which we have not defined in protocol when attempting to deploy a contract there, so we should remove the situation. It also simplifies protocol, it removes these accounts, I feel like the BAL is a cool tool to actually do this transition, it removes no state, it removes no balance, so there should be no user impact. It just cleanups old state the same way how we like to cleanup old code to remove code debt

author: jochembrouwer
content: Not really regarding weird behavior: one can create the accounts with pre spurious dragon with this initcode: `6001600155`(`SSTORE(1,1)`). Leaves account with no code but with non-empty storage and nonce 0. Thus if collission occurs we run initcode on initially non-empty storage

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/608 any EL devs opposed to having the CL decide which gas limit to use for local blocks? The tl;dr why this is useful is 1) only requires users to configure the gas limit on the CL side and 2) allows us to use vanilla ELs as builders, see [comment](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/608#issuecomment-3798868091)
